new_rules.rule: Validate all mandatory governance prerequisites before execution and fail closed when the review configuration is missing, malformed, or below the required roster.
new_rules.do: Before starting a board run, verify the reviewer count is even, at least one full pair exists, each pair is exactly one Codex plus one Claude, and required governance files are present; if any check fails, return a validation error and stop the run.
new_rules.dont: Accept three reviewers, two Codex reviewers in one pair, or a missing component-owned review contract and continue into board execution as if the setup were partially valid.
new_rules.source: Round 0: reviewer-codex:group-1 showed board execution accepted an invalid governed-review roster instead of failing closed.

new_rules.rule: Workflow status vocabularies must be single-sourced and exhaustively implemented across contracts, prompts, types, parsers, and runtime branches; any unhandled status must fail closed.
new_rules.do: When adding a status like `frozen_with_conditions`, update the shared contract, prompt instructions, parser validation, TypeScript union, and the board switch/if branches in the same change, then add an explicit handler for the terminal path.
new_rules.dont: Let the prompt emit `resume_required` or `frozen_with_conditions` while the runtime type union or round loop does not recognize it, causing the board to skip or mis-handle a real terminal state.
new_rules.source: Round 0: reviewer-codex:group-2 and reviewer-claude:group-1 showed leader status values existed in some layers but not others, so terminal-state handling diverged from the governed review-cycle contract.

new_rules.rule: Preserve contract-defined review data end-to-end: carry canonical severities and required audit provenance without lossy remapping, and use safe writes for shared governed artifacts.
applies_to: shared rulebook and contract file writes
new_rules.do: Keep reviewer severities as `blocking|major|minor|suggestion` through fix-decision handling, emit the contract audit block with `component`, `job_id`, `round`, `review_mode`, `provider`, `model`, `latency_ms`, `fallback_used`, and `artifact_refs`, and write shared rulebooks with a lock or atomic swap.
new_rules.dont: Downgrade or rewrite severities during repair processing, emit ad-hoc audit JSON that omits provider/model/latency/fallback provenance, or overwrite a shared rulebook directly with no locking or atomic write strategy.
new_rules.source: Round 0: reviewer-codex:group-3 showed severity propagation, audit evidence, and shared-file update behavior drifted from the shared review contract.

new_rules.rule: Shared governed workflow logic must have one canonical implementation, and all live consumers must import that implementation instead of maintaining diverging local copies or orphaned mirrors.
new_rules.do: Export a single `buildReviewerSummaryText()` from the shared review engine and import it everywhere, and either route `agent-role-builder` through the shared repair engine or delete the unused shared repair path.
new_rules.dont: Keep separate local `buildReviewerSummaryText()` copies or a local `runRepairPass()` beside an unused shared engine, so fixes land in one code path while production continues using another.
new_rules.source: Round 0: reviewer-codex:group-4 and reviewer-claude:group-3/group-4 showed duplicated shared review and repair logic caused mirrored-runtime drift.

existing_rules_covering.explanation: This is a guard-condition mistake in terminal-state handling: the freeze predicate treated any repair item as material work and therefore blocked `frozen` on minor/suggestion findings. IMP-003 already requires testing conditional guards against all terminal states and not just the happy path.

Hardcoding artifact presence to `true` makes the postmortem claim evidence that was not mechanically verified. IMP-002 already requires a rule-by-rule self-check based on actual evidence rather than confidence or placeholders.

no_rule_needed.reason: This group is a bundle of low-level defects rather than one new reusable principle. The silent-failure parts are already covered by IMP-007/IMP-016, while the remaining `arbitration_used` plumbing and fallback-write details are too specific to justify a standalone rule.
